text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 55-year-old female with known stage IV melanoma, BRAF V600E-mutated, on targeted therapy (Tafinlar/Mekinist). Restaging evaluation.

TECHNIQUE:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: Multidetector CT acquired with intravenous contrast in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal reformats generated.
MRI brain: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain performed at 3T with and without gadolinium contrast, including axial T1, T2, FLAIR, DWI, and post-contrast T1 sequences with thin-slice gadolinium-enhanced imaging.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain approximately 6 months prior.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

FINDINGS:

CHEST:
Lungs: No new pulmonary nodules or masses identified. No pleural effusions. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. Mild dependent atelectasis at the lung bases bilaterally.
Mediastinum and hila: No new mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. Heart size within normal limits. No pericardial effusion.
Chest wall and soft tissues: Two new small subcutaneous nodules are identified along the right lateral thigh/upper leg (partially included on inferior pelvis images), measuring approximately 0.7 cm and 0.5 cm in greatest dimension. These were not present on prior imaging and are new from the previous study.
Axilla: There is new right axillary lymphadenopathy with a cluster of enlarged nodes, the largest measuring approximately 1.6 cm in short axis. These were not present on prior imaging.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is moderately enlarged. There has been substantial interval change compared to the prior scan. Previously, the hepatic lesions had shown marked reduction in size and number on the 3-month follow-up study. The current examination demonstrates definite interval increase in the size and number of hepatic metastatic deposits. The dominant lesion in the right lobe has enlarged and now measures approximately 4.2 cm in greatest dimension (previously measured approximately 3.1 cm). Additional known lesions throughout the right and left hepatic lobes are similarly enlarged compared to the most recent prior. Furthermore, there are at least three new hypodense hepatic lesions not previously identified, distributed throughout both lobes, the largest of which measures approximately 1.8 cm. Overall hepatic burden is substantially increased compared to the prior study.
Biliary: No biliary ductal dilation. Gallbladder appears unremarkable.
Spleen: Splenomegaly, stable in appearance. No focal splenic lesions.
Pancreas: Unremarkable. No ductal dilation or peripancreatic stranding.
Kidneys and adrenal glands: Bilateral kidneys are symmetric in size and enhancement pattern. No new renal lesions. Adrenal glands appear within normal limits bilaterally.
Aorta and vasculature: No aneurysmal change. No evidence of venous thrombosis on the images available.
Bowel: Bowel loops are non-distended. No pneumatosis or free air. No obstruction.

PELVIS:
Lymphadenopathy: A rounded left inguinal lymph node is identified measuring approximately 2.8 cm in short axis, which appears new compared to the prior examination. There is also a small new left external iliac node, measuring approximately 1.1 cm, borderline by size criteria. No pelvic free fluid.
Bladder: Unremarkable.
Uterus/Ovaries: Not well visualized on this study. No discrete pelvic masses identified.

BONE WINDOWS:
No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified. No acute fracture.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

FINDINGS:

Brain parenchyma: The cerebral hemispheres, cerebellum, and brainstem demonstrate no major areas of abnormal signal on FLAIR or T2-weighted sequences. No mass effect or midline shift. Ventricles are symmetric and of normal caliber. No hydrocephalus.

Enhancing lesions: Two new small foci of nodular enhancement are identified that were not present on the comparison study:

1. Right frontal lobe: A small rounded focus of smooth, nodular enhancement is seen in the right frontal white matter, measuring approximately 0.8 cm in greatest dimension. There is no surrounding T2/FLAIR signal abnormality and no associated mass effect.

2. Left cerebellar hemisphere: A second small enhancing nodule is identified in the lateral left cerebellar hemisphere, measuring approximately 0.6 cm. Again, no surrounding edema and no mass effect on the fourth ventricle.

Both lesions demonstrate homogeneous enhancement on post-contrast sequences. There is no hemorrhage associated with either lesion on susceptibility-weighted imaging. No additional suspicious foci of enhancement are seen on thin-slice post-contrast sequences.

Dural venous sinuses: Patent on post-contrast sequences. No dural-based enhancement.
Calvarium and skull base: No osseous lesions.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Interval increase in size and number of hepatic metastatic deposits compared to the prior study, with the dominant right hepatic lesion now measuring approximately 4.2 cm. At least three new hepatic lesions are identified, consistent with hepatic disease that has progressed from the previous nadir.

2. New right axillary lymphadenopathy and new enlarged left inguinal node (2.8 cm), not present on prior examination.

3. Two new small subcutaneous nodules along the right lateral thigh/upper leg, new from prior.

4. Two new small enhancing brain lesions on MRI, right frontal lobe (0.8 cm) and left cerebellum (0.6 cm), without surrounding edema or mass effect, most consistent with new metastatic deposits in this clinical context. No prior intracranial disease was present.

5. Overall, findings are consistent with interval disease progression compared to prior imaging, with new intracranial involvement.

Results communicated to the ordering provider at time of finalization.

Dictated and authenticated electronically.
Attending Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
